title: Fix selinux perms in Makefile binary build
body: As a new contributor I required this change to my environment.

comments.body: Yes, I seem to remember hitting that… but please just use `-v $$(pwd):`… , we don’t need an extra variable.

> we don’t need an extra variable.

ACK. Updated.
Personally I now use pet containers for development work which have all of the build dependencies installed, and notably here have SELinux disabled because I'm using overlayfs on my workstation.

You can now run SELinux and overlayfs together if you use a kernel from this repo.

```
cat /etc/yum.repos.d/_copr_pcmoore-kernel-secnext.repo 
[pcmoore-kernel-secnext]
name=Copr repo for kernel-secnext owned by pcmoore
baseurl=https://copr-be.cloud.fedoraproject.org/results/pcmoore/kernel-secnext/fedora-rawhide-$basearch/
skip_if_unavailable=True
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://copr-be.cloud.fedoraproject.org/results/pcmoore/kernel-secnext/pubkey.gpg
enabled=1
```
@aweiteka waiting on tests to merge, thx
